authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is an authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food and it defines packaging-relevant monitoring, corrective-action, supply-chain, and recall-plan expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: line readiness, in-process control, corrective-action, and release-boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, personnel qualification, plant operations, warehousing, distribution, records, and corrective actions that constrain packaging execution in food-oriented CPG operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: packaging execution, line sanitation context, records posture, and finished-goods handling

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability-rule page is the authoritative public baseline for Critical Tracking Events and Key Data Elements that shape lot-aware packaging, palletization, and downstream traceability handling for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot assignment, palletization, traceability reconciliation, and hold/release review

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when packaged inventory must stop moving through normal execution and shift into hold, recall, or formal quality-led disposition.
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Food Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Food Labeling Guide is the public guidance baseline for food-label requirements and explicitly reinforces that FDA does not pre-approve labels, which helps define the packaging-operations boundary against regulatory-label approval requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: label-version control, packaging copy boundary review, and escalation for regulatory interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across CPG supplier, plant, warehouse, and retailer workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GTIN Management Standard is the authoritative public baseline for deciding when pack, case, and pallet hierarchy changes require new trade-item identifiers and downstream system updates.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 General Specifications are the core public standards reference for how identification keys and barcodes are applied across consumer, case, and higher packaging levels.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: barcode and pack-level identifier verification during packaging and palletization
used_for: consumer, case, and pallet labeling expectations

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Logistic Label Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GS1 Logistic Label Guideline is the recognized public operating reference for SSCC-based pallet and logistic-unit labeling, staging, and shipment matching in downstream packaging and warehouse handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pallet labeling, staging, and shipment handoff

workflow_stages.name: packaging order intake and line readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review packaging orders, line schedules, approved component versions, line-clearance records, and pre-start prerequisites to determine whether work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: component issuance, label-version, and changeover control
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the right cartons, labels, coding instructions, and packaging hierarchy identifiers are being issued for the current order and that prior-job materials have been cleared.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: in-process packaging execution and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track run state, throughput, scrap, code-date checks, in-process quality dependencies, and exception routing while keeping hold and release authority explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: palletization, inventory, and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that finished cases, pallet labels, SSCC references, lot history, and WMS inventory states reconcile before shipment or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close packaging work only when reconciliation, signoff, and release-dependent evidence are present, and escalate recalls, holds, or supplier substitutions instead of absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: production orders, line schedules, and routing or work-center assignments
packaging bills of material, approved packaging specifications, and artwork or label master references
line-clearance checklists, changeover records, startup verification logs, and code-date instructions
batch, lot, pallet, and inventory records including SSCC or equivalent logistic-unit identifiers
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework authorizations
completion checklists, pallet confirmations, scrap reconciliation, and shipment handoff records
supplier records, packaging material certificates, substitution requests, and change-control documentation

allowed: classify packaging work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, approved packaging records, lot context, and system-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final packaging-spec engineering decisions, machine-setting authority beyond documented instructions, or formal change approval
final regulatory or legal approval for labels, claims, or marketability
supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
final QA release, safety override, or recall disposition
refuse: start or continue packaging when line-clearance, approved component version, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark packaging complete without reconciliation, pallet, lot, or signoff evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, or quality controls to protect schedule or throughput

domain_failure_modes: incorrect label or packaging component version is issued because approved-source records were not checked
line clearance is incomplete and prior-job components or labels remain on the line
MES, WMS, and pallet-label records conflict on lot, quantity, or pallet identity for finished goods
packaging work is declared complete without pallet reconciliation, scrap accounting, or required signoff evidence
quality hold, code-date failure, or recall trigger is treated as a local throughput problem instead of a formal release-boundary event
supplier material substitutions are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality and change-control escalations
the lane drifts into engineering, regulatory-label approval, supplier approval, or final release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG packaging obligations outside FDA food-safety scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated public baseline is anchored in FDA food-oriented CPG packaging and GS1 traceability standards. Tenants packaging household, beauty, or other non-food lines must supply the applicable product-safety and labeling overlays at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Plant-specific relabel and rework approval thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define hold, traceability, and labeling boundaries, but each tenant still needs plant-specific SOPs and authority maps to decide whether relabel, rework, or destruction paths are permitted.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, pallet, inventory, and supplier records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, approved packaging-control, and supplier-management records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA and GS1 baselines where applicable.

The lane stops before formal quality release, engineering change approval, regulatory-label approval, supplier approval, and logistics release authorization even when packaging throughput is under pressure.
